name: OKX API
what_it_does: Provides a comprehensive v5 REST and WebSocket API for OKX's spot, futures, options, and DeFi products with unified account management across all asset classes.
use_cases: Multi-asset algorithmic trading across spot, perpetuals, options, and structured products
Real-time market data streaming for tick-level price feeds
Unified portfolio management with cross-margin accounts
Earning yield via OKX's DeFi and earn products programmatically
Institutional-grade block trading and RFQ workflows
not_for: US retail users (OKX geo-blocks US IPs)
Simple spot-only use cases where a simpler API suffices
best_when: Building sophisticated trading agents that need access to one of the broadest crypto product suites (spot, derivatives, options, structured products, DeFi) on a single platform.
avoid_when: US-facing deployments or when regulatory clarity is required; also overkill for simple market data-only use cases.

auth.notes: API key + secret + passphrase with HMAC-SHA256. Supports read-only, trade, and withdrawal permission scopes. IP whitelist supported. Simulated trading environment available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Public endpoints unlimited; private endpoints rate-limited per endpoint (varies 5-60 req/2s)
pricing.paid_tiers: VIP tiers based on 30-day trading volume and OKB holdings — lower fees and higher rate limits
pricing.notes: API access free; spot trading fees from 0.08% maker/0.10% taker (base tier); substantial fee discounts for high volume or OKB staking

performance.rate_limits: Per-endpoint limits: typically 5-60 req/2s for private endpoints; public market data higher; WebSocket strongly recommended for streaming

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Comprehensive error code reference in docs; unified v5 API reduces confusion vs legacy endpoints; some error messages lack remediation guidance
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: clOrdId (client order ID) supports idempotent order placement; must be explicitly set by the agent to avoid duplicate orders on retry
known_agent_gotchas: Three-part credential (key + secret + passphrase) increases auth setup complexity
HMAC timestamp must be within 30 seconds of server time
clOrdId must be set for safe retries to prevent duplicate order submission
Account mode (simple, single-currency, multi-currency) affects endpoint behavior — agents must know account type
WebSocket connections require heartbeat (ping every 30s) to stay alive
Simulated trading environment available but separate API endpoint — good for agent testing
US IPs blocked; agent deployment geography matters

security_score_components.security_notes: HMAC-SHA256 with 30s timestamp window; three-factor credential; granular permission scopes; IP whitelist available; dedicated simulated trading environment
